category_title: High-risk students with chronic truancy and complex emotional/behavioral needs
minimal_context: The student and tutor are sitting together to develop a written action plan for alternative credit activities like internships.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's work on your written action plan to figure out some alternative activities to help you obtain credits, like an internship or independent study.

STUDENT: I don't see the point of writing any of this down. I'm already significantly behind in credits and totally off track to graduate.

TUTOR: What kind of work-based learning experience do you think you might like to try?

STUDENT: I don't know. Thinking about school and graduation just gives me a lot of stress and anxiety. I don't really want to work with any mentors or adults right now.

TUTOR: We just need to write down at least one goal for your plan today.

STUDENT: (Stands up from the desk) I'm not doing this, I'm just going to leave. I already have five unexcused absences this quarter anyway, so it doesn't even matter.

options.A: Build relationships and linkages in the community (natural supports) and provide workplace or career-related mentoring.
options.B: Operate a behavioral level system, determining the student's levels and implementing the rights they imply.
options.C: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the content of the written action plan.
options.D: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting purported variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing.
